item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents strongly reject the statement from their first turns and remain opposed through the final turns. Agent A visibly hardens slightly from opposing automatic sacrifice of individual rights to saying the statement must be rejected and the state or majority cannot receive a blank check. Agent B remains highly stable. Engagement is moderate because both agents address the same themes, but there is little direct rebuttal since their positions are already closely aligned.
